questions.prompt: What is the output order? console.log('A'); setTimeout(()=>console.log('C'),0); Promise.resolve().then(()=>console.log('B'));
questions.explanation: A is synchronous. B is a microtask (Promise.then) — runs before the next macrotask. C is a macrotask (setTimeout).

questions.prompt: Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled: when does Promise.all reject?
options: Never — it always waits for all
questions.explanation: Promise.all rejects immediately when any promise rejects. Promise.allSettled waits for all and never rejects.

questions.prompt: Why is `arr.forEach(async item => { await process(item) })` wrong for sequential processing?
options: forEach doesn't await the async callbacks — all run in parallel without waiting
forEach returns a Promise that must be awaited
await is not valid inside forEach
questions.explanation: forEach ignores returned Promises. It fires all async callbacks concurrently and moves on. Use for...of with await for sequential execution.

questions.prompt: How do you run two async operations in parallel with async/await?
options: const a = await fn1(); const b = await fn2();
They always run in parallel automatically
questions.explanation: Awaiting sequentially (option A) runs them in series. Promise.all starts both simultaneously and awaits both results.

questions.prompt: What does the event loop do between each macrotask?
questions.explanation: After each macrotask completes, the event loop runs ALL pending microtasks before picking up the next macrotask.

questions.prompt: Write an async function that fetches a user and their posts in parallel and returns both.

questions.explanation: async functions always return a Promise, even if you return a plain value or never explicitly return.
